title: Sync — Local-First Overlay (Merge-on-Login, Approach A)
deliverable: lib/sync.js fully implementing PRD §4.4: merge-on-login push/pull, optimistic writes while authed, pending-queue offline flush, first-login default-chain seeding, conflict resolution rules. Same queue on phone and desktop after sign-in; offline edits sync back on reconnect.

in: lib/sync.js — primary deliverable implementing the entire sync surface
merge-on-login: push local queue_items, done_items, chains to Supabase; pull remote state; three-way dedupe; write result back to localStorage
first-ever-authed-login: seed 7 default chains for the user if none are present in remote
optimistic local-first write: every queue/chain/done mutation writes localStorage first, then fires async Supabase upsert/delete
pending-queue mechanism: operations enqueued in localStorage['niyet_pending'] while offline-authed; flushed in FIFO order on reconnect
conflict resolution: show_count last-write-wins (Supabase profiles row wins on merge); queue order from position column
online/offline detection via navigator.onLine + window events 'online'/'offline'
hooks/useQueue.js and hooks/useChains.js integration points — call sync functions from these hooks
show_count sync: persist to profiles table on change; read back on merge
App.jsx: invoke mergeOnLogin() after auth session is confirmed (Block 17 onAuthStateChange callback)
out: default chains constant definition (Block 13, already defined in lib/chains.js or hooks/useChains.js)
Service worker background sync (no use of Background Sync API — pending queue is foreground-flush only)
Conflict resolution UI — no user-facing merge conflict dialog; algorithm is deterministic

files.purpose: Primary sync module: mergeOnLogin, optimistic write wrappers (syncQueueAdd, syncQueueRemove, syncQueueReorder, syncChainUpsert, syncChainDelete, syncDoneAdd, syncDoneClear, syncShowCount), pendingQueue enqueue/flush, online/offline listener bootstrap, seedDefaultChains helper
Add/verify helpers used by sync.js: getAll(), setAll() accepting { queue, done, chains, show_count } atomically; ensure keys are stable constants exported (KEYS object)
Route all queue mutations through sync.js wrappers instead of calling storage.js directly; pass authed-user context
Route all chain mutations through sync.js wrappers; trigger seedDefaultChains check on first login
In onAuthStateChange handler (added in Block 17): call sync.mergeOnLogin(user) after SIGNED_IN event; call sync.bootstrapOfflineListener() on mount

implementation_steps.description: Define localStorage key constants and pending-queue shape in lib/storage.js
implementation_steps.details: Export KEYS = { QUEUE: 'niyet_queue', DONE: 'niyet_done', CHAINS: 'niyet_chains', SHOW_COUNT: 'niyet_show_count', PENDING: 'niyet_pending', SEEDED: 'niyet_seeded_uid' }. Add getAll() returning parsed objects for all keys with safe defaults ([], [], [], 1). Add setAll({ queue, done, chains, show_count }) atomically setting all four data keys in one call. These helpers are the only non-sync code needed from storage.js.

implementation_steps.description: Implement PendingQueue — enqueue, drain, and flush in lib/sync.js
implementation_steps.details: PendingQueue stores an array of operation objects in localStorage[KEYS.PENDING]. Shape per item: { id: uuid, type: 'queue_upsert'|'queue_delete'|'chain_upsert'|'chain_delete'|'done_upsert'|'done_clear'|'show_count_upsert', payload: {...}, createdAt: ISO }. enqueuePending(op) appends to the array. flushPending(userId) iterates in FIFO order, executes each op against Supabase, removes successfully-flushed ops one-by-one (so partial failures leave the remainder). On any Supabase error during flush, stop flushing (preserve order) and leave remaining ops in the queue — do not swallow errors silently. Export flushPending.

bootstrapOfflineListener(getUserId) registers window.addEventListener('online', handler) once (guard with a module-level boolean to prevent double-registration). The handler calls flushPending(getUserId()) if getUserId() returns a non-null user id. bootstrapOfflineListener should be called from App.jsx useEffect on mount. navigator.onLine is checked synchronously before any Supabase call in optimistic write wrappers — if false, skip Supabase call and enqueue instead.

Checks localStorage[KEYS.SEEDED] for userId. If the stored value matches userId, returns immediately (idempotent — fast path). Otherwise: fetch chains from Supabase for userId. If none of the 7 fixed default-chain UUIDs are present (check by id, not name), bulk-insert the missing defaults from the DEFAULT_CHAINS constant (Block 13) with user_id = userId, is_default = true, position = index. The 7 fixed UUIDs are: b89e0940-9583-4803-9210-d73924e6cfa4 (Namaz Hazırlığı), 16064f10-a06a-4945-8340-5d0277ebe288 (Odak Bloğu), 17d995ea-0b2f-4110-b3c5-8ff06778a4bb (Sabah Demiri), f905b229-be82-43c5-a267-6f9c38d5c038 (Abdest), c1ba5c76-b02b-4f5b-bd85-cfe11ab91b1f (Uyku Hazırlığı), 3a519d0d-f060-4f1b-be5f-efec4325610a (Evi Topla), 72990e01-d127-410e-88cd-f0c2e3b9edc0 (Güne Başlama). Only insert chains whose UUID is absent in remote (partial seed is safe). On success write localStorage[KEYS.SEEDED] = userId. This function is called inside mergeOnLogin after the initial remote fetch, before the merge step. C1 — locked decision: seed guard deduplicates by these exact UUIDs, shared with Blocks 12/13/14.

implementation_steps.description: Implement mergeOnLogin(user) — the core merge-on-login function
implementation_steps.details: Steps in order: (1) Read local state via storage.getAll(). (2) Call seedDefaultChains(user.id). (3) Fetch all remote rows for this user: SELECT * FROM queue_items, done_items, chains, profiles WHERE user_id = auth.uid(). (4) Push local data not yet in remote: for each local queue item whose id is not found in remote, INSERT into Supabase. Repeat for done_items (match by id). Repeat for chains (match by id). C2 — locked decision: push/dedup uses stable client-assigned id (crypto.randomUUID() from Block 14), NOT text+timestamp. (5) Merge: build merged_queue = dedupe([...localQueue, ...remoteQueue], key = id), preserving lowest position for duplicates, then re-index positions 0..n-1. build merged_done = dedupe([...localDone, ...remoteDone], key = id). build merged_chains = dedupe([...localChains, ...remoteChains], key = id), tiebreak by chains.updated_at — highest updated_at wins (C6 — locked decision). (6) show_count: if profiles row exists use remote show_count; else use local show_count and upsert to profiles. (7) Write merged state to localStorage via storage.setAll(). (8) Return merged state object { queue, done, chains, show_count } so App.jsx can update React state. (9) Flush any pending ops via flushPending(user.id).

implementation_steps.description: Implement optimistic write wrapper — syncQueueAdd(userId, item)
implementation_steps.details: item = { id: uuid, text, position, created_at }. Write to localStorage immediately (append to queue array). If !userId return (anon path, storage-only). If offline (navigator.onLine === false) enqueuePending({ type:'queue_upsert', payload: { user_id: userId, ...item } }) and return. Else call supabase.from('queue_items').upsert({ user_id: userId, ...item }) asynchronously. On Supabase error: enqueuePending the op (do not revert localStorage — optimistic stays).

implementation_steps.description: Implement optimistic write wrappers — syncQueueRemove, syncQueueReorder
implementation_steps.details: syncQueueRemove(userId, itemId): remove from localStorage queue array by id. If authed+online: supabase.from('queue_items').delete().eq('id', itemId).eq('user_id', userId). If authed+offline: enqueue { type:'queue_delete', payload: { id: itemId, user_id: userId } }. syncQueueReorder(userId, orderedItems): orderedItems = [{id, position},...] after drag or pop. Write full reordered array to localStorage. If authed+online: supabase.from('queue_items').upsert(orderedItems.map(i => ({ id: i.id, user_id: userId, position: i.position })), { onConflict: 'id' }). If authed+offline: enqueue { type:'queue_upsert' } for each item with new position.

implementation_steps.description: Implement optimistic write wrappers — syncDoneAdd, syncDoneClear
implementation_steps.details: syncDoneAdd(userId, doneItem): doneItem = { id, text, completed_at, chain_id? }. Append to localStorage done array. If authed+online: supabase.from('done_items').insert({ user_id: userId, ...doneItem }). If authed+offline: enqueuePending. syncDoneClear(userId): clear done array in localStorage. If authed+online: supabase.from('done_items').delete().eq('user_id', userId). If authed+offline: enqueuePending({ type:'done_clear', payload: { user_id: userId } }). The pending flush handler for done_clear must run DELETE before any subsequent done_upsert ops enqueued after it.

implementation_steps.description: Implement optimistic write wrappers — syncChainUpsert, syncChainDelete
implementation_steps.details: syncChainUpsert(userId, chain): chain = { id, name, emoji, steps, position, is_default }. Write to localStorage chains array (upsert by id). If authed+online: supabase.from('chains').upsert({ user_id: userId, ...chain }, { onConflict: 'id' }). If offline: enqueuePending. syncChainDelete(userId, chainId): remove from localStorage. If authed+online: supabase.from('chains').delete().eq('id', chainId). If offline: enqueuePending.

Write show_count to localStorage immediately. If authed+online: supabase.from('profiles').upsert({ id: userId, show_count: count }, { onConflict: 'id' }). If offline: enqueuePending({ type:'show_count_upsert', payload: { id: userId, show_count: count } }). This is last-write-wins — no merge logic needed.

Import { syncQueueAdd, syncQueueRemove, syncQueueReorder, syncDoneAdd, syncDoneClear } from lib/sync.js. Pass the current userId (from auth context or null) to each call. The hook reads from localStorage via storage.js and keeps React state in sync via the returned merged state from mergeOnLogin (passed down from App.jsx as a prop or via context).

Import { syncChainUpsert, syncChainDelete } from lib/sync.js. Replace direct storage calls with sync wrappers. The default-chain seeding is handled inside mergeOnLogin — useChains.js does not need to call seedDefaultChains directly.

implementation_steps.description: Wire mergeOnLogin and bootstrapOfflineListener into App.jsx
implementation_steps.details: In the onAuthStateChange callback (Block 17): on event === 'SIGNED_IN', call const merged = await sync.mergeOnLogin(session.user); then setState({ queue: merged.queue, done: merged.done, chains: merged.chains, showCount: merged.show_count }). In a useEffect on mount: sync.bootstrapOfflineListener(() => currentUser?.id ?? null). Ensure the getUserId lambda always reads the latest ref value — use a useRef for currentUser inside App.jsx.

implementation_steps.description: Implement flushPending handler for done_clear ordering guarantee
implementation_steps.details: In flushPending, when processing a done_clear op, execute the Supabase DELETE for all done_items of that user, then continue to next ops. Subsequent done_upsert ops in the same flush run will insert fresh items — correct order. This ensures Temizle followed by new completions offline results in only the new completions in remote after flush.

implementation_steps.description: Write unit-testable pure merge helpers (no Supabase dependency)
implementation_steps.details: Export three pure functions alongside the async ones: mergeItems(local, remote, keyFn) — returns deduped array using keyFn(item) as identity (keyFn should return item.id per C2), remote wins on duplicate, sorted by created_at asc; mergeChains(local, remote) — dedupe by id (C2), tiebreak by updated_at (higher updated_at wins, per C6), re-index position 0..n-1; reindexPositions(items) — returns array with position = index. These are callable in tests without mocking Supabase.

data_structures.name: PendingOp
data_structures.shape: { id: string (uuid), type: 'queue_upsert'|'queue_delete'|'chain_upsert'|'chain_delete'|'done_upsert'|'done_clear'|'show_count_upsert', payload: object, createdAt: string (ISO 8601) }
data_structures.notes: Stored as JSON array at localStorage['niyet_pending']. Payload shape varies by type: queue_upsert payload matches queue_items row shape; queue_delete payload is { id, user_id }; done_clear payload is { user_id }; show_count_upsert payload is { id (user_id), show_count }.

data_structures.name: MergedState
data_structures.shape: { queue: QueueItem[], done: DoneItem[], chains: Chain[], show_count: number }
data_structures.notes: Return value of mergeOnLogin(). All arrays are fully deduplicated and position-reindexed. This is the canonical state App.jsx should commit to React state after login.

data_structures.name: QueueItem (localStorage + Supabase)
data_structures.shape: { id: string (uuid), text: string, position: number, created_at: string (ISO) }
data_structures.notes: In localStorage the user_id is omitted (implicit). In Supabase it includes user_id. Merge key = id (stable crypto.randomUUID() assigned at creation in Block 14). C2 — locked: text+timestamp is NOT the primary key; id is authoritative. Legacy fallback note: items created before Block 14 UUID enforcement may lack a stable id — handle gracefully by falling back to text+'|'+created_at only for such legacy items.

data_structures.name: DoneItem (localStorage + Supabase)
data_structures.shape: { id: string (uuid), text: string, completed_at: string (ISO), chain_id?: string|null }
data_structures.notes: Merge key = id (stable crypto.randomUUID() assigned at creation in Block 14). C2 — locked: id is authoritative; text+completed_at is NOT the primary key. Legacy fallback note: items without a stable id may fall back to text+'|'+completed_at. chain_id optional — only set when task was completed as part of a chain load.

data_structures.name: Chain (localStorage + Supabase)
data_structures.shape: { id: string (uuid), name: string, emoji: string, steps: string[], position: number, is_default: boolean, created_at?: string, updated_at?: string }
data_structures.notes: Merge key = id (C2 — locked). On id collision tiebreak by updated_at — higher updated_at wins (C6 — locked, chains.updated_at column added in Block 16). Default chains use the 7 fixed hardcoded UUIDs (C1). After merge, positions are re-indexed 0..n-1 sorted by original position then name.

interfaces.name: mergeOnLogin
interfaces.signature: async function mergeOnLogin(user: { id: string }): Promise<MergedState>
interfaces.purpose: Full merge-on-login sequence: seed defaults if first login, push local orphans up, pull remote, three-way dedupe, write back to localStorage, flush pending queue. Called once from App.jsx on SIGNED_IN event.

interfaces.name: bootstrapOfflineListener
interfaces.purpose: Registers window 'online' event listener (once, guarded). On reconnect calls flushPending(getUserId()). Call from App.jsx useEffect on mount.

interfaces.name: flushPending
interfaces.purpose: Drains localStorage pending queue in FIFO order. Executes each op against Supabase. Stops on first error, leaving remaining ops. Safe to call multiple times.

interfaces.name: syncQueueAdd
interfaces.signature: async function syncQueueAdd(userId: string | null, item: QueueItem): Promise<void>
interfaces.purpose: Optimistic local write + Supabase upsert or pending enqueue. userId null = anon, skip remote.

interfaces.name: syncQueueRemove
interfaces.signature: async function syncQueueRemove(userId: string | null, itemId: string): Promise<void>
interfaces.purpose: Remove from localStorage and Supabase (or enqueue delete if offline).

interfaces.name: syncQueueReorder
interfaces.signature: async function syncQueueReorder(userId: string | null, orderedItems: Array<{id: string, position: number}>): Promise<void>
interfaces.purpose: Batch-upsert new position values to Supabase after queue reorder.

interfaces.name: syncDoneAdd
interfaces.signature: async function syncDoneAdd(userId: string | null, doneItem: DoneItem): Promise<void>
interfaces.purpose: Append completed item to localStorage done array and Supabase done_items.

interfaces.name: syncDoneClear
interfaces.signature: async function syncDoneClear(userId: string | null): Promise<void>
interfaces.purpose: Clear done array in localStorage and DELETE all done_items for user in Supabase (Temizle action).

interfaces.name: syncChainUpsert
interfaces.signature: async function syncChainUpsert(userId: string | null, chain: Chain): Promise<void>
interfaces.purpose: Create or update a chain in localStorage and Supabase.

interfaces.name: syncChainDelete
interfaces.signature: async function syncChainDelete(userId: string | null, chainId: string): Promise<void>
interfaces.purpose: Delete chain from localStorage and Supabase.

interfaces.name: syncShowCount
interfaces.signature: async function syncShowCount(userId: string | null, count: number): Promise<void>
interfaces.purpose: Persist show_count to localStorage and profiles table. Last-write-wins, no merge.

interfaces.name: mergeItems
interfaces.signature: function mergeItems(local: object[], remote: object[], keyFn: (item: object) => string): object[]
interfaces.purpose: Pure helper: dedupe two arrays by keyFn (use item => item.id per C2), remote wins on collision. Testable without Supabase.

interfaces.name: mergeChains
interfaces.signature: function mergeChains(local: Chain[], remote: Chain[]): Chain[]
interfaces.purpose: Pure helper: dedupe chains by id (C2), tiebreak by updated_at — higher updated_at wins (C6), re-index positions.

interfaces.name: seedDefaultChains
interfaces.purpose: Insert 7 default chains into Supabase if their fixed UUIDs are absent. Idempotent via localStorage[KEYS.SEEDED] guard and remote UUID check. C1 — locked: dedup by the 7 hardcoded UUIDs shared with Blocks 12/13/14.

acceptance_criteria: After signing in on device A (with local queue items), device B can sign in and see the same queue items after calling mergeOnLogin
No tasks are lost during merge-on-login when both devices had independent local items
Duplicate tasks (same text + created_at within ±1 second) appear only once after merge
Completing a task while offline (authed) writes to localStorage immediately; after reconnect the done_item appears in Supabase
Adding a queue item while offline (authed) writes to localStorage immediately; after reconnect the queue_item appears in Supabase
Temizle (done clear) while offline enqueues a done_clear op; after reconnect Supabase done_items are cleared
New tasks added AFTER a Temizle while still offline are preserved in Supabase after flush (done_clear executes before done_upsert ops in the pending queue)
First authenticated login seeds exactly 7 default chains in Supabase; a second login does not duplicate them
If remote already has chains with the same names as defaults, seeding is skipped entirely
show_count set on device A is reflected on device B after mergeOnLogin (remote wins on merge)
Queue order (position column) is preserved across sync — items appear in the same order on both devices
Anonymous users (no userId) are unaffected by sync module — only localStorage is touched
Supabase upsert errors do not revert localStorage state — optimistic writes persist locally
Calling flushPending multiple times is safe and idempotent (no double-inserts) due to upsert semantics
mergeItems and mergeChains are pure functions testable without any Supabase mock
verification: Manual: add 3 items on desktop (anonymous), sign in → open app on phone → sign in → confirm same 3 items appear in same order
Manual: add item on phone while in airplane mode (authed) → restore connectivity → confirm item appears in Supabase (check via Supabase dashboard Table Editor)
Manual: hit Temizle while offline → complete 2 more tasks offline → reconnect → confirm done_items in Supabase has only the 2 new items
Manual: sign in fresh (new user) → confirm 7 default chains present in Supabase chains table with is_default = true
Manual: sign in a second time (same user) → confirm chains count has not doubled
Manual: set show_count = 3 on desktop while authed → sign into phone → confirm show_count = 3 after mergeOnLogin
Manual: add a chain on device A (authed), then add a different chain on device B (authed, both offline), reconnect A then B → confirm both chains present after each flush
Console: confirm no errors logged during normal authed operation (add, complete, clear cycles)
Code review: mergeItems and mergeChains have at least 3 test cases each covering: empty local + remote, empty remote, duplicate collision, disjoint sets
Network tab: confirm each sync wrapper fires exactly one Supabase request per operation (no redundant calls)

questionable_decisions.decision: Merge key for queue_items and done_items is text + created_at (ISO, second-precision ±1s window)
questionable_decisions.assumption: Two items with identical text submitted within the same second on two devices are the same item. The app does not currently support sub-second rapid re-addition of identically-worded steps.
alternatives: Use the UUID id as the merge key — simpler, but fails when anon localStorage items lack a Supabase-assigned id
Use text alone — risks false deduplication of legitimately repeated steps (e.g. 'su iç' added twice on purpose)
Assign UUIDs client-side at creation time (Block 14) and use id as key — correct if all blocks already generate stable UUIDs at creation
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Check Block 14: if localStorage items already carry stable client-generated UUIDs (gen_random_uuid equivalent via crypto.randomUUID()), use id as the merge key for both push and dedupe. This eliminates the timestamp ambiguity entirely. Switch to text+timestamp only as a fallback for items created before UUIDs were enforced.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C2 — locked: dedupe by stable crypto.randomUUID() id (Block 14 confirmed). text+timestamp retained only as legacy fallback for pre-UUID items.

questionable_decisions.decision: Pending queue stops flushing on the first Supabase error
questionable_decisions.assumption: Ops are order-dependent (especially done_clear before done_upsert) and partial application is worse than full retry. Network errors are transient; the next 'online' event will retry the full remaining queue.
alternatives: Skip erroring op and continue flushing remaining ops — risks out-of-order execution
Retry the failing op up to N times with exponential backoff before stopping
Separate the queue by table (per-table flush queues) so a chains error doesn't block a queue_items flush
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Accept stop-on-first-error for v1. Add per-op retry count (max 3) to PendingOp shape now so retries can be added in Phase 2 without a schema change. Per-table queues add complexity that isn't justified until Realtime (Phase 2) anyway.

questionable_decisions.decision: Remote chain data wins on merge collision (dedupe by name, remote authoritative)
questionable_decisions.assumption: The user is more likely to have edited a chain on another device (remote) than to have a meaningfully newer local-only edit. The merge happens only at login, not continuously.
alternatives: Local wins — simpler, but loses edits made on device B when logging into device A
Last-modified-at timestamp on chains — correct but requires adding updated_at column to chains table (not in PRD §4.3 schema)
Prompt user to choose — unacceptable friction for ADHD users per design principles
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Add updated_at timestamptz to the chains table (default now(), updated via trigger or explicit set on upsert). Use it as the conflict tiebreaker: higher updated_at wins. This is a one-line schema addition. Without it, remote-wins is the least-bad silent choice.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C6 — locked: chains table has updated_at timestamptz (added in Block 16). Tiebreak by updated_at — highest updated_at wins. Blind remote-wins is replaced by this deterministic timestamp comparison.

questionable_decisions.decision: seedDefaultChains guard stored in localStorage (niyet_seeded_uid) rather than checking Supabase
questionable_decisions.assumption: A Supabase round-trip to check chain count on every login adds latency to the merge-on-login critical path. The local guard is sufficient because: (a) it is per-user-id, not global; (b) if localStorage is cleared, the fallback is to check remote chain count before inserting (mergeOnLogin already fetches chains in step 3).
alternatives: Check remote chain count every login — correct but adds one extra SELECT per login
Use a seeded column on the profiles table — most robust but requires schema change
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep the localStorage guard as primary, but in seedDefaultChains always fetch remote chain count as the authoritative check (step 4.3 already fetches chains — reuse that result). The localStorage flag is then a fast-path skip only when remote count > 0 was confirmed on a previous login. This makes it correct even after localStorage clear.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C1 — locked: seed guard deduplicates by the 7 fixed hardcoded UUIDs (shared verbatim with Blocks 12/13/14). Skip inserting any default chain whose UUID already exists in remote. This is UUID-based, not count-based, so partial seeds are handled correctly. localStorage[KEYS.SEEDED] remains the fast-path short-circuit.

open_questions: Do Block 14 localStorage items already carry stable client-generated UUIDs (crypto.randomUUID())? If yes, the merge key should be id, not text+timestamp — see Q1.
Does the Supabase schema (Block 16) include an updated_at column on chains? If not, should it be added before Block 18 is built to enable timestamp-based chain conflict resolution?

What is the expected behaviour when a user clears localStorage (e.g. browser privacy wipe) and then reconnects while authed? Should mergeOnLogin pull full remote state even when local is empty? (Current spec: yes — local is empty so remote state wins entirely, no deletions are mirrored.)
Should done_items older than N days be excluded from the merge pull (pagination / trim) to avoid unbounded localStorage growth for long-term users?
Is there a maximum size concern for the pending queue? Should a cap (e.g. 500 ops) be enforced with a FIFO eviction strategy?

Block 17 (auth) — does it expose a useAuth() hook or context with { user, session }? sync.js needs to read userId; the integration pattern in 18.13 assumes App.jsx passes userId down or a context is available.
